content.Text:
[Thu 2026-04-23 02:21 PDT] Create a new file `sla-dashboard.html` — an interactive Agent SLA Dashboard for AgentBox. This is a self-contained HTML page (all CSS + JS inline, no external deps except font-family system stack).

## Design
Use the same dark theme as the other pages:
- `--color-bg-deep: #1a1a2e`, `--color-bg-mid: #16213e`, `--color-primary: #00d4ff`, `--color-success: #4ade80`, `--color-warning: #facc15`, `--color-danger: #ef4444`
- System font stack, box-sizing border-box

## Features

### 1. Fleet SLA Overview
- Show 6 simulated agents with uptime %, response time avg, error rate, SLA tier (Gold/Silver/Bronze)
- Each agent card shows a health ring (Canvas arc) colored by status (green ≥99.9%, yellow ≥99%, red <99%)
- Fleet summary bar: total agents, agents in compliance, agents at risk, agents breaching

### 2. SLA Budget Tracker
- Each agent has an "error budget" (e.g., Gold = 0.01% downtime/month = ~4.3 min)
- Visual burn-down bar showing budget consumed vs remaining
- Burn rate indicator: if consuming budget faster than linear pace, show warning
- Forecast: at current burn rate, will budget last the period? Show projected exhaustion date

### 3. Response Time Monitor
- Simulated response time history (last 24 data points per agent)
- Sparkline chart per agent using Canvas
- P50, P95, P99 latency display
- SLA threshold line on the sparkline
- Highlight points that exceeded the SLA threshold

### 4. Breach Prediction Engine
- Based on current error budget burn rate, predict future breaches
- Show "Days Until SLA Breach" countdown for at-risk agents
- Risk classification: Safe (>14 days), Caution (7-14 days), Warning (3-7 days), Critical (<3 days)
- Proactive recommendation for each at-risk agent (e.g., "Scale horizontally", "Investigate error spike", "Enable circuit breaker")

### 5. Incident Timeline
- Simulated incident history with timestamps, severity, duration, affected agents
- Scrollable timeline with color-coded severity dots
- Click to expand details

### 6. Proactive Recommendations Panel
- Autonomous analysis section at bottom
- 5-6 actionable recommendations based on fleet state
- Each rec has severity icon, title, description, recommended action

### 7. Auto-Monitor Mode
- Toggle button to enable live simulation
- When enabled, metrics update every 2 seconds with random fluctuations
- Breach alerts appear as toast notifications when thresholds crossed

## Navigation
Add a back link `← Back to Home` pointing to `index.html` at top.

## File: sla-dashboard.html only
Single self-contained HTML file. All styles in `<style>`, all JS in `<script>`. No imports.

After creating the file, verify syntax: `node -c sla-dashboard.html` won't work for HTML, so just make sure the JS is valid by extracting and checking or just ensure no obvious syntax errors.

Then:
```
git add sla-dashboard.html
git commit -m "feat: Agent SLA Dashboard - autonomous SLA monitoring with uptime tracking, error budget burn-down, breach prediction, response time sparklines, incident timeline, proactive recommendations, and auto-monitor mode"
git push origin HEAD:main
```
I’m creating `sla-dashboard.html` as a self-contained dashboard and first checking the workspace so the page matches the existing structure and theme conventions.

I found existing pages in the same family, including `sla.html`, `metrics-simulator.html`, and `watchdog.html`. I’m reading those now to reuse layout and interaction patterns instead of inventing a conflicting page.

I have enough context to build this in the repo’s existing single-file dashboard style. Next I’m checking the git state, then I’ll add the page with simulated fleet data, canvas-based health rings and sparklines, live burn-rate forecasting, incidents, recommendations, and auto-monitor alerts.

🤖 AgentBox

Service Level Agreement

Effective: March 26, 2026

Contents

  1. Overview
  2. Uptime Commitment
  3. Response Time Targets
  4. Support Tiers
  5. Exclusions
  6. Service Credits & Remedies
  7. Scheduled Maintenance
  8. Monitoring & Reporting
  9. Changes to This SLA

1. Overview

This Service Level Agreement ("SLA") describes the uptime guarantees, performance targets, and support commitments AgentBox provides to its users. This SLA applies to all paid plans; free-tier users receive best-effort service.

2. Uptime Commitment

AgentBox commits to the following monthly uptime targets:

Plan Monthly Uptime Max Downtime / Month
Starter99.5%~3 h 36 min
Pro99.9%~43 min
Enterprise99.95%~22 min

Uptime is calculated as: (Total minutes − Downtime minutes) / Total minutes × 100. Scheduled maintenance windows are excluded from downtime calculations.

3. Response Time Targets

AgentBox targets the following median response latencies for AI agent interactions:

Request Type Target (p50) Target (p95)
Text message< 2 s< 5 s
Image analysis< 4 s< 10 s
Web search< 3 s< 8 s
Reminder / scheduled task< 1 s< 3 s

These are targets, not guarantees. Response times may vary based on upstream AI provider performance.

4. Support Tiers

Severity Description Initial Response Resolution Target
Critical Service completely unavailable 15 minutes 4 hours
High Major feature degraded 1 hour 8 hours
Medium Minor feature issue 4 hours 48 hours
Low General inquiry 24 hours 5 business days

Support is available via email at support@getagentbox.com and through the in-app help chat.

5. Exclusions

The following are not counted as downtime for SLA purposes:

  • Scheduled maintenance (announced ≥ 48 hours in advance)
  • Force majeure events (natural disasters, wars, pandemics)
  • Third-party service outages (Telegram API, AI model providers)
  • User-caused issues (API misuse, exceeding rate limits)
  • Network issues outside AgentBox's infrastructure
  • Beta or experimental features explicitly marked as such

6. Service Credits & Remedies

If AgentBox fails to meet the uptime commitment for a given month, affected paid users may request service credits:

Monthly Uptime Credit (% of Monthly Fee)
99.0% – below target10%
95.0% – 98.99%25%
Below 95.0%50%

Credits must be requested within 30 days of the affected month. Credits are applied to future billing and are not redeemable for cash. Maximum credit per month is capped at 50% of that month's fee.

7. Scheduled Maintenance

Routine maintenance is performed during low-traffic windows (typically Tuesdays 2:00–4:00 AM UTC). Users are notified at least 48 hours in advance via email and the Status Page. Emergency maintenance may occur without advance notice when required to address security vulnerabilities or critical bugs.

8. Monitoring & Reporting

AgentBox uses automated monitoring to track uptime and performance. Current system status is always available on the Status Page. Monthly uptime reports are published in the Changelog.

9. Changes to This SLA

We may update this SLA from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email at least 30 days before they take effect. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated SLA.

Questions? Contact us at support@getagentbox.com.

← Back to Home · Terms of Service · Privacy Center · System Status
